steps.title: Identify the pattern between these age groups
steps.content: The question asks for a conclusion “best supported” by the table—meaning verifiable from actual numbers, not inference. Comparing age 50-64 with age 30-49: personal relationships increase (58% vs. 53%) while finances decrease (56% vs. 63%). This reversal is the decisive pattern.

steps.title: Verify B's claim against the data
steps.content:
Choice B correctly states that age 50-64 resolution makers are more likely to choose personal relationships (58% vs. 53%) and less likely to choose finances (56% vs. 63%) than age 30-49 makers.

The other answers either assume unsupported overlap, confuse conditional percentages with population statistics, or reverse a comparison.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
